_meta.source_document: Candela GentleMAX Operator's Manual, PN 8501-00-1810 Revision 06, June 2007
_meta.extraction_method: PyMuPDF full-text + clinical applications-specialist review, page-cited
_meta.citation_rule: Every value carries the manual page it came from. Values NOT present in this manual (per-indication recommended settings) are recorded in `gaps` and MUST render locked in the tool, never fabricated.
_meta.critical_finding: This is a device/safety manual. It contains real device SPECIFICATION envelopes (fluence ranges, DCD ranges, spot sizes, limits) and full safety content, but deliberately DEFERS all per-indication/per-Fitzpatrick recommended treatment settings to a separate document (Candela Clinical Treatment Guidelines P/N 8502-00-0907, not in corpus). Recommended-setting fields are therefore source-UNsupported and render locked.

wavelengths.type: Dual-wavelength, optically combined, single delivery system

notes.text: WARNING: no currently approved indications for 1.5 mm and 3 mm spot sizes at 755 nm
Alexandrite fluence table lists only 6,8,10,12,15,18 mm; Nd:YAG table lists all incl. 1.5,3 mm

fluence_tables_Jcm2._note: Real fluence CAPABILITY ranges from the p73 tables, by wavelength x spot size x pulse-duration band. These are device envelopes / limits, NOT recommended treatment doses.

pulse_width_ms.capability: 0.25 to 300 ms (spec table states 0.35-300; control text & Table 3-6 state 0.25-300)
pulse_width_ms.spec_inconsistency_flag: Min pulse width printed as 0.35 ms (p154) vs 0.25 ms (p77,105). Surface both; do not silently pick one.

repetition_rate_hz.capability: Single, or 0.5 to 10 Hz depending on spot size / pulse duration

dcd_cooling.value: HFC 134a / GentleCool, 1000 g canister, dual-canister auto-switch

safety_specs.name: Fiber bend radius
safety_specs.value: never pulse below 6 in (15 cm) radius

treatment_parameters_recommended._status: ABSENT FROM THIS MANUAL — renders LOCKED
treatment_parameters_recommended.reason: Candela defers recommended fluence/pulse/DCD/spot per indication and Fitzpatrick to Clinical Treatment Guidelines P/N 8502-00-0907 and on-device Guided Mode software; not printed here.

contraindications.text: Personal history of skin cancer, such as melanoma
Infected 'target' tattoo site or adjacent areas
Using medication for which infra-red light is a contraindication

warnings_precautions.text: Class 4 laser: protective eyewear required for all persons in the treatment room during operation
Match spot size on lens cartridge/distance gauge to the control-panel spot size or improper energy is delivered
Always recalibrate after changing delivery system/handpiece/lens/windows; failure may deliver excessive energy
Too-short cryogen spray duration for a given spot size may cause patient burns
Spot overlap may cause crescent and general burns
Tilting the distance gauge produces an elliptical spot and crescent burns; hold perpendicular
Replace cryogen canister when prompted or reduced epidermal cooling may cause burns
Flash-fire hazard in O2-enriched atmosphere; shave/keep hair-bearing areas moist; avoid alcohol/acetone prep
Cryogen ventilation: keep area <1000 ppm; min 40 sq ft room; rooms <513 sq ft need >=130 CFM exhaust
Frostbite distances (Table 1-3): 27 in visual from canister; 19 in visual from handpiece tip
Caution with 1.5 mm and 3.0 mm spots at 755 nm — no currently approved indications

guided_mode_inputs._note: The device's Guided Mode collects these to pick 'commonly used settings' — but the resulting numbers live in software/Treatment Guidelines, not this manual.

gaps: No per-indication recommended treatment settings (deferred to Clinical Treatment Guidelines P/N 8502-00-0907).
No fluence-by-Fitzpatrick tables (inputs feed Guided Mode software; numbers not printed).
No recommended pulse duration / DCD / rep-rate / spot per indication.
No number of passes, treatment intervals, or fluence-titration guidance.
No clinical endpoints or expected tissue responses.
Pulse-width min inconsistency: 0.35 ms (spec table) vs 0.25 ms (control text).
assessment: Authoritative for device operating envelope + hard limits + full safety layer. Empty of per-indication clinical doses by design. A source-locked tool can confidently show real fluence/DCD/spot/pulse capability ranges and safety gates, and must render recommended-setting fields LOCKED with 'not specified in operator manual' rather than any number.
